content.problemStatement:
Alice has quarreled with Berta recently. Now she doesn't want to have anything in common with her!

Recently, they've received two collections of positive integers. The Alice's integers were **A1**, **A2**, ..., **AN**, while Berta's were **B1**, **B2**, ..., **BM**.

Now Alice wants to throw away the minimal amount of number from her collection so that their collections would have no common numbers, i.e. there won't be any number which is present in both collections. Please help Alice to find the minimal amount of numbers she would need to throw away.
constraints: All numbers are distinct within a single girl's collection.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integer numbers N and M denoting the amount of numbers in Alice's and Berta's collections respectively. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the numbers of Alice. The third line contains M space-separated integers B1, B2, ..., BM denoting the numbers of Berta.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimal amount of numbers Alice needs to throw away from her collection so that she wouldn't have any numbers in common with Berta.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, Alice needs to throw away the number 3 to have no common numbers with Berta. In the second test case, Alice and Berta have no common numbers, so Alice doesn't need to throw away any numbers.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a set intersection approach. We first read the numbers of Alice and Berta into two separate sets. Then, we find the intersection of the two sets, which represents the numbers that are common to both Alice and Berta. The size of this intersection set is the minimal amount of numbers Alice needs to throw away.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N + M)) — where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of numbers in Alice's collection, and M is the number of numbers in Berta's collection. The time complexity is dominated by the set operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + M) — where N is the number of numbers in Alice's collection, and M is the number of numbers in Berta's collection. The space complexity is dominated by the storage of the sets.

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N, M = map(int, input().split())
    A = set(map(int, input().split()))
    B = set(map(int, input().split()))
    print(len(A & B))
